text.title: SSL/TLS Management Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the SSL/TLS Management methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Cloudflare SSL mode MUST be 'Full (strict)' — never 'Flexible'. The origin nginx MUST present a valid cert (Cloudflare origin OR Let's Encrypt).
rule.rationale: Flexible serves HTTP to the origin while showing HTTPS to the browser — a classic MITM-friendly misconfiguration.

rule.statement: Every cert has an expiry monitor (cron + alert) firing at 14d to expiry. No silent expirations.
rule.rationale: Let's Encrypt auto-renewal fails (DNS, rate limits, hook errors); monitoring is the safety net.

rule.statement: ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3 only. ssl_ciphers from Mozilla 'Intermediate' preset. ssl_prefer_server_ciphers off (TLS 1.3 ignores it; 1.2 client preference is fine for Intermediate).
rule.rationale: TLS 1.0/1.1 are deprecated; legacy ciphers (RC4, 3DES, CBC) carry known weaknesses.

rule.statement: Every TlsPlan names a single owner accountable for renewal. 'team' / 'we' / '' are forbidden.
When mode=letsencrypt, ssl_stapling on + ssl_stapling_verify on + ssl_trusted_certificate set. When mode=cloudflare-origin, stapling is OFF (CF handles it).
rule.rationale: OCSP soft-fail is the default; explicit verified stapling is the operator-controlled posture.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-ssl-tls-management.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
